AION Labs in Israel, backed by Pfizer, Teva, AstraZeneca, and Merck, has raised about US$40M to date. It stands out as a model for consortium-based AI drug discovery.
Tempus raised US$200M+ in 2024 at a valuation of US$8.1B and has continued to expand its clinical partnerships into 2025. Its valuation remains steady, reflecting both the promise and the challenges of scaling healthcare AI.
Insilico Medicine raised a US$95M Series D in 2024 at a US$1.5B valuation and continues to grow its pipeline of AI-discovered molecules.
Compared to 2024, capital inflows remain high, but growth in valuations has been slower, reflecting regulatory timelines and the need for clinical validation before broader adoption.
4. Manufacturing & Industry 4.0
Industrial AI continues to attract capital, though the sector remains smaller in deal volume compared to infrastructure and security.
Applied Intuition raised US$600M in mid-2025, reaching a valuation of US$15B . This is a significant step up from its prior valuations and underscores investor conviction in simulation and ADAS/robotics software.
Vayyar Imaging in Israel raised US$108M in 2024, reaching a valuation of US$1.5B , leveraging its AI sensors across automotive and industrial applications.
Bright Machines raised US$100M in 2024 at a valuation of US$1.1B , focused on AI-powered robotics and manufacturing automation.
Relative to 2024, deal sizes are larger but concentrated. Growth continues in simulation and robotics, though early-stage activity has cooled somewhat, especially in Israel.
5. Retail & Consumer
Retail and consumer AI remain active, with incumbents continuing to buy startups and private capital flowing into generative tools.
Wix acquired Base44 for US$80M in 2025, bringing conversational app-building capabilities into its SME-focused platform.
Runway raised US$141M at a valuation of US$1.5B in 2023 and continues to grow, cementing its role as one of the leading AI creative platforms.
Character AI has raised US$190M total (including its US$150M Series A in 2023) at a valuation of about US$1B . In 2025, it is exploring either a new round or a sale as it grapples with rising infrastructure costs. Revenue has climbed toward US$30–50M annualized , showing early traction but not yet on the scale of other unicorns.
Compared to 2024, deal activity is slightly down, but average valuations remain high for the leading players.
6. Cybersecurity & Defense
This vertical has had some of the most significant deals of 2025, with valuations climbing sharply.
Scale AI saw Meta acquire a 49% stake for about US$14.8–15B , valuing the company at ~US$29B . While not a full acquisition, the transaction makes Scale one of the most strategically important AI companies for defense and infrastructure.
CrowdStrike acquired Pangea Cyber for US$260M in 2025 to add AI-enhanced detection and response.
Wiz’s US$32B acquisition by Google officially closed in 2025, making it the largest cybersecurity transaction in AI history to date.
Compared to 2024, capital and M&A values are both significantly higher, reflecting the urgency of AI-driven security.
7. Transportation & Mobility
AI in transportation has seen fewer but larger deals, with capital clustering around autonomy and logistics.
Wayve in the UK is negotiating a US$500M raise in 2025, with Nvidia among potential backers. This represents a large step up from its prior Series B.
Aurora raised US$800M in 2025 as a public company, helping stabilize its financial position amid ongoing uncertainty in the AV sector.
Nuro raised US$600M in its Series D at an US$8.6B valuation , continuing to push forward in autonomous delivery.
Compared to 2024, overall deal volume is lower, but the size of transactions is larger, with investors focusing capital on a small group of autonomy leaders.
V. Underpenetrated Sectors & White Space
While many verticals are crowded or well capitalized, several sectors remain underinvested relative to their size and potential. These are opportunities for early-stage ventures.
Construction & Real Estate : Project risk modeling, AI for site safety, materials supply chain optimization, building performance analytics. Many firms remain manual; a few startups exist but few large exits and limited capital density.
Agriculture & Food Systems : Precision farming, disease detection, yield optimization using drone/satellite imagery; post-harvest sorting automation. Adoption is constrained by small farm scale, distribution, climate variability.
Energy & Utilities : Grid optimization, renewable energy forecasting, predictive maintenance of infrastructure, demand response AI. Regulatory and technical inertia slow growth.
Hospitality & Tourism : Guest experience automation, dynamic pricing, review analytics, operational scheduling; human service remains central and sensitive to brand risk.
Government & Education : Budget constraints, procurement risk, equity / regulatory concerns; digital infrastructure and policy make or break adoption; still mostly pilots rather than scale.
These sectors are risky (long time to scale, unclear regulatory or revenue models) but for investors willing to accept slower growth, they may deliver outsized returns as vertical AI matures.
VI. Strategic Investors & Corporate Adoption
Key players are not just startups—they include large corporations, venture funds, and government entities making strategic bets, and acquiring capabilities, competing on infrastructure, or embedding AI.
Corporate Acquisitions and Bets
Investor Landscape
Corporate Adoption Patterns
VII. Future Outlook & Thesis
Drawing together the data, deal flow, barriers, and vertical dynamics, the following points form the forecast and investment thesis for Vertical AI over the next 3-7 years.
Foundational Infrastructure Will Cement Leadership Compute, data pipelines, domain-adapted models, safe/aligned AI will separate winners. Firms that invest early in interpretability, safety, compliance, edge deployment will likely capture premium.
Acceleration from Pilot to Production Though many current uses are pilots or internal tools, the pressure (competitive, regulatory, cost) will force enterprises to scale. Vertical AI becoming core to operations in regulated industries will drive growth.
Valuations Remain High (Frothy), But Metrics Will Matter More Investors are paying up for growth, scale, perceived future dominance. However, future rounds and exits will reward those with strong unit economics, retention, domain like safety/regulatory evidence, deployed customers, real revenue traction—not just hype.
Acquisition & Partnerships will Increase Many large SaaS players, industrial firms, defense contractors, and incumbents lack AI native capabilities. Acquiring startups that provide vertical-specific intelligence or infrastructure will be a common strategy. Partnerships (e.g. embedding startup models into enterprise platforms) will also become more frequent.
White-Space Verticals Will Emerge as Key Sources of Innovation As frontrunners saturate certain verticals, under-penetrated sectors like construction, agriculture, utilities, gov/education will become increasingly attractive, especially for investors with domain knowledge and regulatory understanding.
Regulation, Ethics, Data Governance will be Determinants As AI proliferates, regulatory scrutiny will increase. Privacy laws, model bias, explainability, safety, licensing of training data / content all will matter. Startups and investors who build governance early will have competitive advantage.
Returns Delayed, Risk Elevated Many current startups will struggle or fail. Infrastructure heavy companies may burn cash waiting for enterprise contracts; those in difficult verticals may find market resistance. But for those reaching scale, vertical AI may generate multi-billion or (in aggregate) trillion dollar value flows, with AI becoming as pervasive as cloud or the internet.

title_original: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised ...
body_original:
Image Credits: sankai / Getty Images
AI
Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
Tim Fernholz
9:00 AM PDT · April 1, 2026
The most advanced silicon chips have accelerated the development of artificial intelligence. Now can AI return the favor?
Cognichip is building a deep learning model to work alongside engineers as they design new computer chips. The problem it is trying to solve is one the industry has lived with for decades: Chip design is enormously complex, ruinously expensive, and slow. Advanced chips take three to five years to go from conception to mass production; the design phase alone can take as long as two years before physical layout begins. Consider that the latest line of Nvidia GPUs, Blackwell, contains 104 billion transistors — that’s a lot to line up.
In the time it takes to create a new chip, Cognichip CEO and founder Faraj Aalaei says the market can change and make all that investment a waste. Aalaei’s goal is to bring the kind of AI tools that software engineers have used to speed their work into the semiconductor design space.
“These systems have now become intelligent enough that by just guiding them and telling them what the result is that you want, it can actually produce beautiful code,” Aalaei told TechCrunch.
He says the firm’s technology can reduce the cost of chip development by more than 75% and cut the timeline by more than half.
The company emerged from stealth last year and said Wednesday that it had raised $60 million in new funding led by Seligman Ventures, with notable participation from Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan, who will be joining Cognichip’s board. Umesh Padval, a managing partner at Seligman, will also join the board. Cognichip has now raised $93 million altogether since its founding in 2024.
Still, Cognichip can’t yet point to a new chip designed with its system and did not disclose any of the customers it says it has been collaborating with since September.
The company says its advantage is in using its own model trained on chip design data, rather than starting with a general-purpose LLM. That required getting access to domain-specific training data, which is no small feat. Unlike software developers, who share vast amounts of code openly, chip designers guard their IP closely, making the kind of open source trove that typically trains AI coding assistants largely unavailable.
Cognichip has had to develop its own datasets, including synthetic data, and license data from partners. The firm has also developed procedures to allow chipmakers to securely train Cognichip’s models on their own proprietary data without exposing it.
Where proprietary data isn’t available, Cognichip has leaned on open source alternatives. In one demo last year, Cognichip invited electrical engineering students at San Jose State University to try the model in a hackathon. The teams were able to use the model to design CPUs based on the RISC-V open source chip architecture — a freely available design that anyone can build on.
Cognichip is competing against incumbent players like Synopsys and Cadence Design Systems, as well as well-funded startups like ChipAgents, which closed a $74 million extended Series A in February, and Ricursive, which raised a $300 million Series A round in January.
Padval said that the current flood of capital into AI infrastructure is the largest he’s seen in 40 years of investing.
“If it’s a super cycle for semiconductors and hardware, it’s a super cycle for companies like [Cognichip],” he said.
Topics
AI , Exclusive , Hardware , Venture
When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission . This doesn’t affect our editorial independence.
Tim Fernholz
Senior Reporter
Tim Fernholz is a journalist who writes about technology, finance and public policy. He has closely covered the rise of the private space industry and is the author of Rocket Billionaires: Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos and the New Space Race. Formerly, he was a senior reporter at Quartz, the global business news site, for more than a decade, and began his career as a political reporter in Washington, D.C.
You can contact or verify outreach from Tim by emailing tim.fernholz@techcrunch.com or via an encrypted message to tim_fernholz.21 on Signal.

Looking for recently funded AI startups? This AI startup database tracks 9,953+ verified companies with founder emails, funding details, and decision-maker contacts updated every week. AI startups raised $42.97 billion in 2024 and are on pace to surpass that in 2025, making them the highest-value targets in B2B sales right now.
Our B2B lead database for AI startups includes verified CEO and founder emails, funding round details, investor information, and company intelligence — everything your sales team needs to reach decision-makers at companies with fresh capital to spend.
Below, you’ll find 100 recently funded AI startups with actionable data you can use today.
Take me to the AI startups now 👉
Get Leads Every Week
Order a Custom List
Table of Contents
The Hottest AI Startups Right Now
The AI startup landscape is constantly evolving, with new companies emerging as leaders based on funding momentum, product innovation, and market traction. Our list highlights the hottest AI companies of 2025—companies that have recently secured significant funding rounds and are showing exceptional growth potential.
What makes an AI startup “hot”? We consider recent funding velocity (companies raising large rounds in the past 3-6 months), breakthrough technology that solves real problems, strong founder teams with proven track records, and early customer traction showing product-market fit. The startups in our database represent the most exciting opportunities across generative AI, enterprise software , healthcare , infrastructure, and emerging AI applications.
Many of these companies began their journey with pre-seed or seed funding , progressing through Series A , Series B , and later stage rounds as they prove market demand and scale their technology. Understanding where these AI companies are in their funding journey helps investors identify the right entry points and entrepreneurs benchmark their own progress.
What Makes a Startup “AI”?
The term “AI startup” has become increasingly broad, but our database focuses on companies that meet specific criteria. We track startups that are either:
Building foundational AI technology – Companies developing large language models , computer vision systems, machine learning infrastructure, or AI chips
Creating AI-native products – Startups where AI is the core product functionality, not just a feature add-on
Deploying AI for industry transformation – Companies using AI to fundamentally reimagine workflows in healthcare , finance , manufacturing , legal , and other sectors
We specifically exclude companies that simply integrate existing AI APIs without proprietary technology or those that market themselves as “AI-powered” without substantive AI implementation. This careful curation ensures our database focuses on genuine AI innovation rather than marketing buzzwords.
For context on related technologies, explore our lists of robotics startups , data analytics startups , and cloud computing startups that often complement AI technology stacks.
Why This AI Startup Database Is Different
Unlike aggregator sites that simply scrape press releases, our Growth List database provides:
Double-verified email addresses for 7,380 key contacts including founders, CEOs, and decision-makers
Real-time funding updates – Our team updates this list weekly with the latest funding rounds
X & LinkedIn social profiles connecting you directly to startup leadership
Dozens of additional data points including company stage, employee count, technology stack, and growth metrics
Geographic and industry segmentation making it easy to find AI startups in your target market
Every entry is manually verified against multiple data sources, including Crunchbase , company announcements, and SEC filings to ensure accuracy. This verification process sets our database apart from automated data aggregators that often contain outdated or incorrect information.
Our subscribers also gain access to investor information, allowing you to identify which AI-focused venture capital firms are backing specific companies and track funding patterns across the AI ecosystem. manually verified against multiple data sources, including Crunchbase , company announcements, and SEC filings to ensure accuracy.
AI Startup Funding Trends in 2025
The AI startup landscape is experiencing a notable shift in funding patterns. While the total funding volume remains strong, we’re seeing several key trends emerge that reshape how capital flows to AI companies.
Concentration in established players : Mega-rounds ($100M+) are increasingly going to AI companies that have already proven product-market fit and revenue traction, particularly in enterprise AI and AI infrastructure. Companies like xAI’s massive $20 billion Series E demonstrate this trend toward backing winners at scale.
Sector diversification : Beyond the initial wave of generative AI and LLM startups, funding is flowing into specialized vertical AI applications in healthcare diagnostics, legal tech, supply chain optimization, and climate technology . This diversification mirrors broader market maturation as investors seek defensible moats through domain expertise.
Geographic expansion : While Silicon Valley and San Francisco still dominate AI startup funding, we’re tracking significant growth in AI hubs across Austin , New York , London , Paris , Tel Aviv , Singapore, and Toronto . Each region brings unique strengths and regulatory environments that influence AI development.
Earlier-stage caution : Seed and pre-seed AI startups are facing more scrutiny than in 2023-2024, with investors demanding clearer differentiation and paths to monetization beyond “GPT wrapper” applications. The bar for early-stage funding has risen considerably as the market matures.
For those building companies in this space, understanding funding benchmarks across stages is crucial. Compare our AI startup data with SaaS startups and B2B software companies to contextualize valuation expectations.
How to Use This AI Startup List
Our database of recently funded AI startups is designed for multiple use cases:
For Investors : Identify emerging AI companies in your thesis area, track funding velocity, and discover startups before they become overheated. Filter by funding stage, geography, and industry to build your target list. Cross-reference with our AI VC investors list to understand competitive dynamics and co-investment opportunities.
For Job Seekers : Find fast-growing AI companies that are hiring. Recent funding is one of the strongest indicators of expansion and new job opportunities. Connect directly with founders through our verified contact information. Explore opportunities across cybersecurity startups , healthcare tech , and other AI-adjacent sectors.
For Business Development : Discover potential AI technology partners, vendors, or acquisition targets. Our database includes contact details for decision-makers, making outreach more efficient. Companies selling B2B lead generation tools and services can prioritize recently funded AI startups with fresh capital to spend.
For Market Research : Analyze AI startup trends, identify emerging technology categories, and understand where capital is flowing in the AI ecosystem. Track geographic concentrations and sector-specific funding patterns to inform strategic decisions.
For Founders : Research your competitive landscape, identify potential acquirers or strategic partners, and learn from the positioning and funding strategies of similar AI companies. Benchmark your progress against peers at similar stages and in comparable verticals.
Get Leads Every Week
Order a Custom List
Recently Funded AI Startups
Below you’ll find our curated list of the 100 most recently funded AI startups, updated weekly. Each entry includes the company name, website, primary industry focus, headquarters location, funding amount, round type, and date of last funding.
For a comprehensive view of all startup categories organized by location and funding stage, explore our complete startup lists guide . You can also browse startups by specific locations like NYC , Boston , Los Angeles , Chicago , Seattle , or international markets .

Looking for recently funded AI startups? This AI startup database tracks 9,953+ verified companies with founder emails, funding details, and decision-maker contacts updated every week. AI startups raised $42.97 billion in 2024 and are on pace to surpass that in 2025, making them the highest-value targets in B2B sales right now.
Our B2B lead database for AI startups includes verified CEO and founder emails, funding round details, investor information, and company intelligence — everything your sales team needs to reach decision-makers at companies with fresh capital to spend.
Below, you’ll find 100 recently funded AI startups with actionable data you can use today.
Take me to the AI startups now 👉
Get Leads Every Week
Order a Custom List
Table of Contents
The Hottest AI Startups Right Now
The AI startup landscape is constantly evolving, with new companies emerging as leaders based on funding momentum, product innovation, and market traction. Our list highlights the hottest AI companies of 2025—companies that have recently secured significant funding rounds and are showing exceptional growth potential.
What makes an AI startup “hot”? We consider recent funding velocity (companies raising large rounds in the past 3-6 months), breakthrough technology that solves real problems, strong founder teams with proven track records, and early customer traction showing product-market fit. The startups in our database represent the most exciting opportunities across generative AI, enterprise software , healthcare , infrastructure, and emerging AI applications.
Many of these companies began their journey with pre-seed or seed funding , progressing through Series A , Series B , and later stage rounds as they prove market demand and scale their technology. Understanding where these AI companies are in their funding journey helps investors identify the right entry points and entrepreneurs benchmark their own progress.
What Makes a Startup “AI”?
The term “AI startup” has become increasingly broad, but our database focuses on companies that meet specific criteria. We track startups that are either:
Building foundational AI technology – Companies developing large language models , computer vision systems, machine learning infrastructure, or AI chips
Creating AI-native products – Startups where AI is the core product functionality, not just a feature add-on
Deploying AI for industry transformation – Companies using AI to fundamentally reimagine workflows in healthcare , finance , manufacturing , legal , and other sectors
We specifically exclude companies that simply integrate existing AI APIs without proprietary technology or those that market themselves as “AI-powered” without substantive AI implementation. This careful curation ensures our database focuses on genuine AI innovation rather than marketing buzzwords.
For context on related technologies, explore our lists of robotics startups , data analytics startups , and cloud computing startups that often complement AI technology stacks.
Why This AI Startup Database Is Different
Unlike aggregator sites that simply scrape press releases, our Growth List database provides:
Double-verified email addresses for 7,380 key contacts including founders, CEOs, and decision-makers
Real-time funding updates – Our team updates this list weekly with the latest funding rounds
X & LinkedIn social profiles connecting you directly to startup leadership
Dozens of additional data points including company stage, employee count, technology stack, and growth metrics
Geographic and industry segmentation making it easy to find AI startups in your target market
Every entry is manually verified against multiple data sources, including Crunchbase , company announcements, and SEC filings to ensure accuracy. This verification process sets our database apart from automated data aggregators that often contain outdated or incorrect information.
Our subscribers also gain access to investor information, allowing you to identify which AI-focused venture capital firms are backing specific companies and track funding patterns across the AI ecosystem. manually verified against multiple data sources, including Crunchbase , company announcements, and SEC filings to ensure accuracy.
AI Startup Funding Trends in 2025
The AI startup landscape is experiencing a notable shift in funding patterns. While the total funding volume remains strong, we’re seeing several key trends emerge that reshape how capital flows to AI companies.
Concentration in established players : Mega-rounds ($100M+) are increasingly going to AI companies that have already proven product-market fit and revenue traction, particularly in enterprise AI and AI infrastructure. Companies like xAI’s massive $20 billion Series E demonstrate this trend toward backing winners at scale.
Sector diversification : Beyond the initial wave of generative AI and LLM startups, funding is flowing into specialized vertical AI applications in healthcare diagnostics, legal tech, supply chain optimization, and climate technology . This diversification mirrors broader market maturation as investors seek defensible moats through domain expertise.
Geographic expansion : While Silicon Valley and San Francisco still dominate AI startup funding, we’re tracking significant growth in AI hubs across Austin , New York , London , Paris , Tel Aviv , Singapore, and Toronto . Each region brings unique strengths and regulatory environments that influence AI development.
Earlier-stage caution : Seed and pre-seed AI startups are facing more scrutiny than in 2023-2024, with investors demanding clearer differentiation and paths to monetization beyond “GPT wrapper” applications. The bar for early-stage funding has risen considerably as the market matures.
For those building companies in this space, understanding funding benchmarks across stages is crucial. Compare our AI startup data with SaaS startups and B2B software companies to contextualize valuation expectations.
How to Use This AI Startup List
Our database of recently funded AI startups is designed for multiple use cases:
For Investors : Identify emerging AI companies in your thesis area, track funding velocity, and discover startups before they become overheated. Filter by funding stage, geography, and industry to build your target list. Cross-reference with our AI VC investors list to understand competitive dynamics and co-investment opportunities.
For Job Seekers : Find fast-growing AI companies that are hiring. Recent funding is one of the strongest indicators of expansion and new job opportunities. Connect directly with founders through our verified contact information. Explore opportunities across cybersecurity startups , healthcare tech , and other AI-adjacent sectors.
For Business Development : Discover potential AI technology partners, vendors, or acquisition targets. Our database includes contact details for decision-makers, making outreach more efficient. Companies selling B2B lead generation tools and services can prioritize recently funded AI startups with fresh capital to spend.
For Market Research : Analyze AI startup trends, identify emerging technology categories, and understand where capital is flowing in the AI ecosystem. Track geographic concentrations and sector-specific funding patterns to inform strategic decisions.
For Founders : Research your competitive landscape, identify potential acquirers or strategic partners, and learn from the positioning and funding strategies of similar AI companies. Benchmark your progress against peers at similar stages and in comparable verticals.

AI SaaS Market Overview
The global AI SaaS market size was valued at USD 22.21 billion in 2025. The market is projected to grow from USD 30.33 billion in 2026 to USD 367.6 billion by 2034, exhibiting a CAGR of 36.59% during the forecast period.
The AI SaaS Market represents software-as-a-service platforms that embed artificial intelligence capabilities into cloud-delivered applications. These solutions enable organizations to access advanced analytics, automation, prediction, and decision-support tools without building complex in-house AI infrastructure. The AI SaaS Market Analysis highlights strong adoption across enterprises seeking scalable, cost-efficient, and continuously updated AI functionality. AI SaaS platforms support use cases such as customer analytics, process automation, fraud detection, personalization, and operational optimization. The AI SaaS Industry Report reflects growing reliance on subscription-based AI models that integrate seamlessly with enterprise systems. Flexibility, rapid deployment, and continuous model improvement position AI SaaS as a core component of modern digital transformation strategies.
The United States AI SaaS market is driven by advanced cloud infrastructure, strong enterprise digitalization, and widespread adoption of AI-driven business applications. Organizations across technology, finance, healthcare, retail, and manufacturing deploy AI SaaS solutions to improve productivity, automate workflows, and enhance customer engagement. The AI SaaS Market Insights indicate high demand for machine learning–powered analytics, natural language processing tools, and AI-driven automation platforms. Strong innovation ecosystems and early adoption culture support continuous development. Enterprises favor AI SaaS for scalability and rapid integration. The United States remains a leading region for AI SaaS platform development and enterprise adoption.
Key Findings
Market Size & Growth
Global Market Size 2025: USD 22.21 billion
Global Market Forecast 2034: USD 367.6 billion
CAGR (2025–2034): 36.59%
Market Share – Regional
North America: 38%
Europe: 25%
Asia-Pacific: 27%
Rest of the World: 10%
Country-Level Shares
Germany: 8% of Europe’s market
United Kingdom: 6% of Europe’s market
Japan: 5% of Asia-Pacific market
China: 12% of Asia-Pacific market
AI SaaS Market Latest Trends
The AI SaaS Market Trends highlight rapid evolution driven by cloud maturity, enterprise automation demand, and continuous advances in artificial intelligence models. Organizations increasingly adopt AI SaaS platforms to embed intelligence directly into business applications such as customer relationship management, enterprise resource planning, human resource analytics, and cybersecurity operations. The AI SaaS Market Analysis shows strong momentum toward pre-trained and domain-specific AI models that reduce deployment complexity and shorten implementation timelines.
Download Free sample to learn more about this report.
Another key trend is the rise of generative and conversational AI within SaaS platforms, enabling intelligent virtual assistants, automated content generation, and advanced customer interaction tools. AI SaaS providers are integrating natural language processing and contextual awareness to improve user experience and decision accuracy. The AI SaaS Industry Report also indicates growing adoption of low-code and no-code AI SaaS solutions that empower non-technical users to leverage AI capabilities. Enhanced data governance, privacy controls, and responsible AI frameworks are becoming standard features. These trends collectively strengthen the AI SaaS Market Outlook by expanding enterprise accessibility, scalability, and operational value across industries.
AI SaaS Market Dynamics
DRIVER
Accelerating enterprise demand for intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making
The primary driver of growth in the AI SaaS Market is the accelerating enterprise demand for intelligent automation, predictive analytics, and real-time decision support. Organizations are increasingly relying on AI SaaS platforms to automate routine processes, analyze large data volumes, and generate actionable insights without heavy infrastructure investment. The AI SaaS Market Analysis highlights strong adoption in functions such as customer engagement, finance, operations, marketing, and risk management. AI SaaS solutions enable faster deployment of machine learning and analytics capabilities through cloud-based delivery models. Continuous model updates and scalability further enhance value. As businesses pursue operational efficiency and competitive differentiation, AI SaaS platforms become essential tools for enterprise transformation and productivity enhancement.
RESTRAINT
Data privacy concerns and integration complexity
A major restraint in the AI SaaS Market is concern over data privacy, security, and integration with existing enterprise systems. Organizations handling sensitive data face challenges in ensuring compliance with data protection regulations when using cloud-based AI services. The AI SaaS Market Research Report indicates that data residency requirements and trust issues can slow adoption, particularly in regulated industries. Integration complexity with legacy systems also presents barriers, increasing deployment timelines and costs. Limited transparency in AI model decision-making can further restrict adoption. These factors collectively restrain broader penetration of AI SaaS solutions, especially among organizations with strict governance requirements.
OPPORTUNITY
Expansion of industry-specific and vertical AI SaaS solutions
The AI SaaS Market Opportunities are driven by the expansion of industry-specific AI SaaS platforms tailored to vertical use cases. Providers are increasingly developing domain-focused solutions for healthcare analytics, financial risk assessment, retail personalization, and supply chain optimization. The AI SaaS Market Insights highlight strong demand for configurable AI tools that address specific business challenges rather than generic analytics. Vertical specialization improves accuracy, usability, and adoption speed. Integration with industry workflows enhances value. As enterprises seek targeted AI outcomes, vertical AI SaaS offerings create significant growth opportunities and long-term customer retention potential.
CHALLENGE
Talent dependency and model performance management
One of the key challenges in the AI SaaS Market is dependency on skilled talent to manage model performance, data quality, and continuous optimization. Although AI SaaS reduces infrastructure burden, organizations still require expertise to interpret results and align outputs with business goals. The AI SaaS Industry Analysis highlights challenges related to model drift, bias mitigation, and performance monitoring. Ensuring consistent accuracy across diverse datasets is complex. Vendor accountability and transparency are critical. Addressing these challenges is essential for sustaining trust and maximizing long-term value from AI SaaS deployments.
AI SaaS Market Segmentation
By Technology
Machine learning accounts for approximately 38% market share in the AI SaaS Market. This segment forms the backbone of AI SaaS platforms by enabling predictive analytics, pattern recognition, and automated decision-making. Enterprises deploy machine learning–based SaaS solutions for demand forecasting, fraud detection, recommendation engines, and operational optimization. The AI SaaS Market Analysis highlights strong adoption due to continuous learning capabilities and adaptability across industries. Machine learning models improve accuracy over time using historical and real-time data. Cloud-based delivery supports scalability and cost efficiency. Integration with enterprise systems enhances usability. Automated insights reduce manual analysis. This segment remains dominant due to its versatility and business impact.
Natural language processing represents nearly 27% market share in the AI SaaS Market. NLP-powered SaaS solutions enable text analysis, speech recognition, sentiment analysis, and conversational interfaces. AI SaaS Market Overview
The global AI SaaS market size was valued at USD 22.21 billion in 2025. The market is projected to grow from USD 30.33 billion in 2026 to USD 367.6 billion by 2034, exhibiting a CAGR of 36.59% during the forecast period.
The AI SaaS Market represents software-as-a-service platforms that embed artificial intelligence capabilities into cloud-delivered applications. These solutions enable organizations to access advanced analytics, automation, prediction, and decision-support tools without building complex in-house AI infrastructure. The AI SaaS Market Analysis highlights strong adoption across enterprises seeking scalable, cost-efficient, and continuously updated AI functionality. AI SaaS platforms support use cases such as customer analytics, process automation, fraud detection, personalization, and operational optimization. The AI SaaS Industry Report reflects growing reliance on subscription-based AI models that integrate seamlessly with enterprise systems. Flexibility, rapid deployment, and continuous model improvement position AI SaaS as a core component of modern digital transformation strategies.
The United States AI SaaS market is driven by advanced cloud infrastructure, strong enterprise digitalization, and widespread adoption of AI-driven business applications. Organizations across technology, finance, healthcare, retail, and manufacturing deploy AI SaaS solutions to improve productivity, automate workflows, and enhance customer engagement. The AI SaaS Market Insights indicate high demand for machine learning–powered analytics, natural language processing tools, and AI-driven automation platforms. Strong innovation ecosystems and early adoption culture support continuous development. Enterprises favor AI SaaS for scalability and rapid integration. The United States remains a leading region for AI SaaS platform development and enterprise adoption.

Market Size & Growth
Global Market Size 2025: USD 22.21 billion
Global Market Forecast 2034: USD 367.6 billion

AI SaaS Market Latest Trends
The AI SaaS Market Trends highlight rapid evolution driven by cloud maturity, enterprise automation demand, and continuous advances in artificial intelligence models. Organizations increasingly adopt AI SaaS platforms to embed intelligence directly into business applications such as customer relationship management, enterprise resource planning, human resource analytics, and cybersecurity operations. The AI SaaS Market Analysis shows strong momentum toward pre-trained and domain-specific AI models that reduce deployment complexity and shorten implementation timelines.

Another key trend is the rise of generative and conversational AI within SaaS platforms, enabling intelligent virtual assistants, automated content generation, and advanced customer interaction tools. AI SaaS providers are integrating natural language processing and contextual awareness to improve user experience and decision accuracy. The AI SaaS Industry Report also indicates growing adoption of low-code and no-code AI SaaS solutions that empower non-technical users to leverage AI capabilities. Enhanced data governance, privacy controls, and responsible AI frameworks are becoming standard features. These trends collectively strengthen the AI SaaS Market Outlook by expanding enterprise accessibility, scalability, and operational value across industries.
AI SaaS Market Dynamics
DRIVER
Accelerating enterprise demand for intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making
The primary driver of growth in the AI SaaS Market is the accelerating enterprise demand for intelligent automation, predictive analytics, and real-time decision support. Organizations are increasingly relying on AI SaaS platforms to automate routine processes, analyze large data volumes, and generate actionable insights without heavy infrastructure investment. The AI SaaS Market Analysis highlights strong adoption in functions such as customer engagement, finance, operations, marketing, and risk management. AI SaaS solutions enable faster deployment of machine learning and analytics capabilities through cloud-based delivery models. Continuous model updates and scalability further enhance value. As businesses pursue operational efficiency and competitive differentiation, AI SaaS platforms become essential tools for enterprise transformation and productivity enhancement.
RESTRAINT
Data privacy concerns and integration complexity
A major restraint in the AI SaaS Market is concern over data privacy, security, and integration with existing enterprise systems. Organizations handling sensitive data face challenges in ensuring compliance with data protection regulations when using cloud-based AI services. The AI SaaS Market Research Report indicates that data residency requirements and trust issues can slow adoption, particularly in regulated industries. Integration complexity with legacy systems also presents barriers, increasing deployment timelines and costs. Limited transparency in AI model decision-making can further restrict adoption. These factors collectively restrain broader penetration of AI SaaS solutions, especially among organizations with strict governance requirements.
OPPORTUNITY
Expansion of industry-specific and vertical AI SaaS solutions
The AI SaaS Market Opportunities are driven by the expansion of industry-specific AI SaaS platforms tailored to vertical use cases. Providers are increasingly developing domain-focused solutions for healthcare analytics, financial risk assessment, retail personalization, and supply chain optimization. The AI SaaS Market Insights highlight strong demand for configurable AI tools that address specific business challenges rather than generic analytics. Vertical specialization improves accuracy, usability, and adoption speed. Integration with industry workflows enhances value. As enterprises seek targeted AI outcomes, vertical AI SaaS offerings create significant growth opportunities and long-term customer retention potential.
CHALLENGE
Talent dependency and model performance management
One of the key challenges in the AI SaaS Market is dependency on skilled talent to manage model performance, data quality, and continuous optimization. Although AI SaaS reduces infrastructure burden, organizations still require expertise to interpret results and align outputs with business goals. The AI SaaS Industry Analysis highlights challenges related to model drift, bias mitigation, and performance monitoring. Ensuring consistent accuracy across diverse datasets is complex. Vendor accountability and transparency are critical. Addressing these challenges is essential for sustaining trust and maximizing long-term value from AI SaaS deployments.

OpenAI 已提交 S-1 草案，释放潜在 IPO 上市信号
2026/6/9 5:57:25
来源： IT之家
作者： 故渊
责编： 故渊
评论：
感谢IT之家网友 对的时间点 的线索投递！
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，OpenAI 公司今天（6 月 9 日）发布博文， 宣布已经向美国证券交易委员会递交保密版 S-1 注册草案，为未来上市预留通道。
IT之家注：S-1 是企业向美国证券交易委员会提交的注册声明文件，常见于首次公开募股前。
S-1 文件通常包含公司业务、风险、财务与发行相关信息，提交 S-1 不等于马上上市，但说明企业已为进入公开资本市场做准备。
OpenAI 在博文中坦言，公司提交的这份 S-1 文件迟早会泄露，因此不如大大方方地主动发布消息。
在时间安排上，OpenAI 强调尚未作出上市决定，而且“可能还要一段时间”。
IT之家曾于 6 月 2 日报道，OpenAI 的竞争对手 Anthropic 已秘密递交 IPO 申请，暂未确定发行股份数量与发行定价。Anthropic 表示，本次 IPO 落地与否取决于市场环境及各类综合因素。
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

欧菲光发布机器人 RGBD 相机 Dex400R，可实现手眼协同、无序抓取、近远场避障等功能
2026/6/8 18:26:55
来源： IT之家
作者： 沁沧（实习）
责编： 沁沧
评论：
IT之家 6 月 8 日消息，欧菲光今日宣布，推出 RoboVision 系列的首发产品 Dex400R RGBD（D 指深度 Depth）相机，支持 D2C+IMU 同步、数采、外部触发等模式， 可实现手眼协同、无序抓取、近远场避障等功能 ，为通用机器人场景量身定制。
IT之家获悉，Dex400R 专为机器人机械臂抓取识别、数据采集、补盲避障场景深度定制，通过硬件级同步、全局快门、IMU 融合等设计，保障机器人高速动态作业的稳定性与精准度。
官方表示，Dex400R 尺寸为 45mm*28mm*28mm，近距离 10-20cm 识别精度＜1.5mm，可实现毫米级超高感知精度，远距离误差小于 1%；测距盲区 5cm，近场精密抓取、细缝检测，远场感知全覆盖。
该产品采用 USB+GMSL 双接口设计 ，为同品类业内唯一，兼顾本地直连与长距抗干扰传输，适配工业、机器人多场景部署，拓展更多应用场景。
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

人形机器人天工 3.0 将于下半年开启规模化量产，搭载地瓜机器人旭日 S600 芯片
2026/6/8 23:25:28
来源： IT之家
作者： 沁沧（实习）
责编： 沁沧
评论：
感谢IT之家网友 斯文当不了饭吃 、 江山已旧 、 勤劳的小蜜蜂 的线索投递！
IT之家 6 月 8 日消息，北京人形机器人创新中心（北京人形）与地瓜机器人今日正式公布产业化合作新进展，双方协同打造的 全尺寸通用人形机器人天工 3.0 ，确定于 2026 年下半年 开启规模化量产交付。
据介绍，该机型将正式搭载地瓜机器人 旭日 S600 具身智能大算力芯片，广泛应用于工业制造、商业服务、3D 复杂场景作业等领域。
▲ 旭日 S600 据IT之家此前报道，今年 2 月， 天工 3.0 正式发布 ，配备高扭矩一体化关节实现肢体爆发力， 可翻越 1 米障碍 并完成高爆发连续动作；通过多自由度协同校准将操作精度控制在 毫米级 ，适配工业制造场景需求。
官方表示，依托技术架构与规模化量产优势， 天工 3.0 关键零部件综合成本有望显著降低 。量产落地后的天工 3.0，将全面投入产线作业、仓储物流、智能服务、特殊环境运维等实景应用。
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

title_original: 奥尔特曼宣布 OpenAI 进入第三发展阶段：让 AI 普及、易用且安全
title_zh: 奥尔特曼宣布 OpenAI 进入第三发展阶段：让 AI 普及、易用且安全
body_original:
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，自 OpenAI 推出聊天机器人 ChatGPT、将人工智能带入大众视野至今已有三年半。该公司首席执行官兼联合创始人山姆 · 奥尔特曼表示，企业如今已迈入第三发展阶段：让人工智能普及化、易用化且安全可靠。
IT之家注意到，当地时间本周一，奥尔特曼与 OpenAI 首席科学家雅各布 · 帕霍茨基联合发布博客文章，阐述公司未来发展规划。二人称，OpenAI 第一阶段聚焦通用人工智能的技术研发；第二阶段则面向全球推出产品，并研究用户的实际使用场景。
他们在文中写道：“如今我们正式进入第三阶段。经济格局正围绕人工智能迎来重塑。当下的核心问题是，如何让先进人工智能技术变得供给充足、成本亲民、安全实用、简单易上手，让每个个人与机构都能从中获益。顶尖技术能力只是一部分，更重要的是将技术转化为真正能助力人们发展的实用工具。”
OpenAI 提出三大核心目标：打造自动化人工智能研究员、推动经济提速发展、为全球每个人配备专属通用人工智能。
文中还提到：“强大的智能系统必须坚守安全底线，契合人类意愿，并始终处于人类管控之下。我们并不追求全面自动化的未来，那样的生活缺乏价值，也暗藏风险。人工智能应当助力人们追逐理想，而非脱离人类的掌控。”
二人同时指出，各国及全球层面需开展协同合作，并再次呼吁成立国际机构，专门应对人工智能带来的风险，必要时可暂缓前沿模型的研发进度。
奥尔特曼和帕霍茨基表示：“理想的人工智能未来，绝不能是少数机构把持核心技术与全部发展红利。未来应当让更多个人、企业、社群和国家都能参与技术建设、共享发展成果、掌握相应话语权。”
就在这份规划发布当天，OpenAI 宣布已秘密提交首次公开募股（IPO）申请，但公司坦言，股票正式上市仍需等待较长一段时间。
这篇博文也让人联想到另一家同样筹备上市的头部人工智能企业。上周，人工智能公司 Anthropic 的研究人员发文称，人工智能技术发展速度过快，各大前沿实验室或许应当适当放缓研发脚步。
该文章写道：“我们认为，全球有必要获得暂缓或临时叫停前沿人工智能研发的选择权，从而让社会配套体系与技术安全对齐研究，跟上技术发展的步伐。”
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

他们在文中写道：“如今我们正式进入第三阶段。经济格局正围绕人工智能迎来重塑。当下的核心问题是，如何让先进人工智能技术变得供给充足、成本亲民、安全实用、简单易上手，让每个个人与机构都能从中获益。顶尖技术能力只是一部分，更重要的是将技术转化为真正能助力人们发展的实用工具。”
OpenAI 提出三大核心目标：打造自动化人工智能研究员、推动经济提速发展、为全球每个人配备专属通用人工智能。
文中还提到：“强大的智能系统必须坚守安全底线，契合人类意愿，并始终处于人类管控之下。我们并不追求全面自动化的未来，那样的生活缺乏价值，也暗藏风险。人工智能应当助力人们追逐理想，而非脱离人类的掌控。”
二人同时指出，各国及全球层面需开展协同合作，并再次呼吁成立国际机构，专门应对人工智能带来的风险，必要时可暂缓前沿模型的研发进度。
奥尔特曼和帕霍茨基表示：“理想的人工智能未来，绝不能是少数机构把持核心技术与全部发展红利。未来应当让更多个人、企业、社群和国家都能参与技术建设、共享发展成果、掌握相应话语权。”
就在这份规划发布当天，OpenAI 宣布已秘密提交首次公开募股（IPO）申请，但公司坦言，股票正式上市仍需等待较长一段时间。
这篇博文也让人联想到另一家同样筹备上市的头部人工智能企业。上周，人工智能公司 Anthropic 的研究人员发文称，人工智能技术发展速度过快，各大前沿实验室或许应当适当放缓研发脚步。
该文章写道：“我们认为，全球有必要获得暂缓或临时叫停前沿人工智能研发的选择权，从而让社会配套体系与技术安全对齐研究，跟上技术发展的步伐。”

Arm 介绍 AppReady for Windows：助力开发者加速构建原生 WoA 应用
2026/6/8 19:01:15
来源： IT之家
作者： 溯波（实习）
责编： 溯波
评论：
IT之家 6 月 8 日消息，Arm 在其本月 5 日的一份新闻稿中介绍了 Arm AppReady for Windows。这一由其主导并得到 Microsoft（微软）反馈支持的项目旨在 引导开发者获取相关学习资源 ，并为构建原生 Windows on Arm (WoA) 应用提供支持。
Arm 表示，原生 WoA 应用有助于带来更出色的终端用户体验，并充分释放 Arm 平台的价值。而 AppReady for Windows 则 整合了 AI 赋能工具、开发指导、专家支持 ，帮助开发者降低代码迁移至原生支持的复杂度。
这一项目可帮助开发者检查关键依赖项是否已有 WoA 原生版本；审视构建系统、编译器设置、安装程序及插件；识别与架构相关的代码以及性能关键路径；获取 Arm 与微软提供的 WoA 原生开发指导；探索 AI 赋能的工具和实践，用于扫描代码、识别可移植性问题，并加速移植工作。
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

title_original: Claude 为 Connector 开发者推出性能监控仪表盘
title_zh: Claude 为 Connector 开发者推出性能监控仪表盘
body_original:
Observability for developers building connectors
Developers can now monitor their connector's performance across Claude products and submit connectors to the directory in-app.
Category
No items found.
Product
Claude apps
Date
June 8, 2026
Reading time
min
Share
Copy link https://claude.com/blog/observability-for-developers-building-connectors
Monitor, debug, and improve connectors
Published connectors in the directory now have a dashboard showing how they’re performing across Claude product surfaces. Connector owners can use it to:
Track adoption. Monitor active users, total tool calls, and directory rank over time.
Diagnose errors and latency. See health score, error rates, and latency at a glance, with per-tool error breakdowns to pinpoint what's failing. ‍
Break down usage by product. Compare tool calls across Claude, Claude Code, Cowork, and more to understand where users are engaging.
Stylized view of observability for connectors. Data is illustrative. Available today in public beta. Find it in Claude under Directory in Organization settings . Requires a Team or Enterprise account with Admin or Owner access, or a custom role with the Libraries permission on Enterprise.
Joining the directory
Connectors are built on the Model Context Protocol (MCP) . There are over 300 third-party connectors in the directory , used by millions of people every day. If you wish to submit your MCP server to the directory, you can now do so directly in Claude. Learn more .
No items found.

苹果 iOS 27 升级钱包应用：支持 AI 导入票证
2026/6/9 5:47:21
来源： IT之家
作者： 故渊
责编： 故渊
评论：
感谢IT之家网友 手写的从前 、 对的时间点 的线索投递！
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，科技媒体 9to5Mac 今天（6 月 9 日）发布博文，报道称在 iOS 27 系统中，苹果升级钱包（Apple Wallet）应用， 引入创建票证和用 Apple Cash 分摊账单功能。
创建票证方面，用户在 iOS 27 的 Wallet 应用内点击“＋”按钮后，可以通过以下 2 种方式创建：
通过 iPhone 相机扫描实体票证，再借助视觉智能导入；
手动从零创建数字票证。其中苹果提供了 3 种类型，分别是标准、会员和活动。
另一个功能就是“用 Apple Cash 分摊账单”，用户在相机应用的新 Siri 模式中扫描收据后，系统会提供分摊账单选项。
在打开 Wallet 应用后，苹果会给出 2 种操作路径：向他人发起收款请求，或直接支付自己应承担的份额。IT之家查询公开资料，苹果还优化了 Apple Pay 结账页面：
界面方面，苹果调整了订单功能的入口，此外苹果也为 Wallet 带来新版应用图标。
苹果 WWDC26 全球开发者大会专题

苹果智能和 Siri AI 适配机型公布，iPhone 15 Pro 系列、16 及以后型号可用
2026/6/9 2:59:45
来源： IT之家
作者： 归泷
责编： 归泷
评论：
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，在今日的 WWDC26 主题演讲中，苹果发布了 Siri AI 和多项 Apple 智能新功能。
IT之家注意到，目前苹果官网已公布苹果智能和 Siri AI 适配机型：
苹果智能和 Siri AI 在 iOS 27、iPadOS 27、macOS 27、watchOS 27 和 visionOS 27 中可应用于 iPhone 16 及以后型号、iPhone 15 Pro、iPhone 15 Pro Max、iPad mini（A17 Pro）、搭载 M1 及以上版本的 iPad 型号、搭载 M1 及以上版本的 Mac、Apple Vision Pro、Apple Watch Series 10 及以后版本，Apple Watch Ultra 2 及更新版本，以及与附近支持 Apple Intelligence 的 iPhone 配合时的 Apple Watch SE 3。
另外，苹果最强端侧模型仅限 iPhone Air 、 iPhone 17 Pro 系列手机，以及 M4 iPad 和 M3 Mac 及后续机型。苹果官方特别在发布会上提到，在中国大陆，Siri AI 和其他 Apple 智能新功能需要配合监管要求推进相关工作，因此暂不提供。Siri AI 初期不会在欧盟地区的 iOS 和 iPad OS 提供。

title_original: Meta 智能眼镜暗藏人脸识别代码，曝光仅一天后被紧急移除
title_zh: Meta 智能眼镜暗藏人脸识别代码，曝光仅一天后被紧急移除
body_original:
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，据《连线》杂志报道，就在有人在 Meta 智能眼镜配套应用中发现一段疑似人脸识别算法的休眠代码仅一天后，Meta 便推送更新移除了这段代码。该媒体在核查一款负责智能眼镜部分核心功能的 Meta 人工智能应用代码时，首次发现了这段可疑代码。Meta 内部将其命名为“姓名标签”。简单来说，这款用于通过蓝牙将智能眼镜与手机配对的必备应用，原本还具备一项功能：用户佩戴眼镜时，自动采集沿途所有人的面部影像。
IT之家注意到，《连线》于 6 月 4 日发现了这款处于休眠状态的工具。其内置算法可将人脸照片转化为存储在设备本地的生物特征标识，并会对每一次新的面部扫描信息进行交叉比对。6 月 5 日，Meta 就发布更新，彻底删除了该功能代码。早在今年 2 月，《纽约时报》就曾报道，Meta 正着手为旗下智能眼镜搭载人脸识别功能。彼时该报就称公司内部频繁提及“姓名标签”这一项目代号，由此推测，《连线》此次发现的代码，正是该项目的研发成果。
从这款工具的运行逻辑来看，其初衷或许是帮助用户更轻松地认出旧识。对于记性不好的人而言，这无疑是个便利功能，但用来解决人际交往中记不住人名的常见难题，却显得格外令人不适，还严重侵犯隐私。大多数人宁愿对方坦诚说忘了自己的名字，也不愿自己的样貌被架在眼前的摄像头悄悄采集。
Meta 智能眼镜由公司联合陆逊梯卡集团旗下雷朋、欧克利等知名品牌共同打造。这款产品早已引发诸多争议：不少人利用它偷拍、骚扰女性。去年 12 月，纽约地铁上还发生过一起女子被指损毁他人 Meta 智能眼镜的事件。今年 3 月，瑞典一家媒体经调查曝光，肯尼亚的工作人员会审核这款智能眼镜拍摄的影像，其中包含私密行为、如厕画面等疑似在机主不知情的情况下录制的内容，Meta 也因此遭到集体诉讼。
Meta 通讯副总裁安迪 · 斯通周一向《连线》发表声明称，该功能仅为试点项目，公司尚未就“在此事上采取何种行动”做出“正式决定”。这话或许不假，但确实有 Meta 员工耗费工时、领取薪酬，完成了这段代码的编写、审核，并将其搭载到正式上线的产品中。即便该功能从未启用，也难以安抚两类人群：一是不愿让自己的设备沦为移动数据采集器的眼镜用户，二是那些无意之中面部信息就可能被分析的普通人。Meta 迅速删除代码并发布公关声明，这一行为本身也说明，公司清楚这类侵犯隐私的功能正让其处于危险的边缘。
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

苹果：Siri AI 和其他 Apple 智能新功能在中国大陆暂不提供
2026/6/9 2:14:17
来源： IT之家
作者： 汪淼
责编： 汪淼
评论：
感谢IT之家网友 Wllen 、 仿达 、 tangkin 的线索投递！
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，在今日的 WWDC26 主题演讲中，苹果发布了 Siri AI 和多项 Apple 智能新功能。
官方特别在发布会上提到，在中国大陆，Siri AI 和其他 Apple 智能新功能需要配合监管要求推进相关工作， 因此暂不提供 。
此外，Siri AI 初期不会在欧盟地区的 iOS 和 iPad OS 提供。
根据苹果官方的说法，Siri AI 将在 macOS 27、visionOS 27 和 watchOS 27 上向欧盟用户开放。
但由于《数字市场法案》（DMA），苹果无法在欧盟推出面向 iOS 27 和 iPadOS 27 的 Siri AI。过去几个月， 欧盟监管机构未接受苹果提出的任何方案 。目前尚无 Siri AI 在欧盟 iOS 和 iPadOS 上可用的时间表。
苹果公司表示，按照欧盟监管机构的规定，DMA 要求苹果赋予任何 AI 几乎无限制地访问用户设备的权限，并且能够自主地对该访问权限采取行动，而无需用户持续的可见性和控制。鉴于对用户的严重风险，苹果设计了一种名为可信系统代理（Trusted System Agent）的解决方案 —— 一个中介，允许虚拟助理安全地访问欧盟设备中与 Siri AI 相同的功能和能力。苹果还分享了在欧盟推出 Siri AI 的计划，并在 18 个月内逐步推广这一新方案。 欧盟委员会表示不同意 。
IT之家注意到，硬件配置要求方面，Apple 智能新功能可在当前已支持 Apple 智能的产品机型使用；苹果最强端侧模型仅限 iPhone Air 、 iPhone 17 Pro 系列手机，以及 M4 iPad 和 M3 Mac 及后续机型。
苹果 WWDC26 全球开发者大会专题

投票：苹果 iOS 27 正式公布，你尝鲜了吗？还满意吗？
2026/6/9 7:55:27
来源： IT之家
作者： 汪淼
责编： 汪淼
评论：
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，苹果今日举行 WWDC26 主题演讲， 正式公布了 iOS 27 系统更新 ，带来 Siri AI 和新一代 Apple 智能，并对系统性能进行了大幅优化和可靠性改进。
看完发布会的IT之家小伙伴一定有一个感受，那就是 iOS 27 在“求稳”，兑现了拖延已久的手机 AI 功能，并且优化了液态玻璃，也很良心地支持了 iPhone 11。
此外，iOS 27 对中国用户也有特别照顾， 终于补足了中国大陆节假日调休闹钟 ， 并且中文输入法也进行了优化 。
不过，作为发布重点的 Siri AI 和新一代 Apple 智能 暂不在中国大陆提供 ，让不少熬夜等候的小伙伴有一种“白看了”的感觉。
iOS 27 开发者预览版 Beta 1 目前已面向全球用户推出 ，公测版将于 7 月推出，正式版将于秋季推出，大家有没有第一时间升级尝鲜呢？对于 iOS 27 是否满意呢？欢迎投票。

Xcode 27 Beta 发布：苹果称其是构建 AI 智能体应用的“最佳场所”
2026/6/9 3:18:57
来源： IT之家
作者： 故渊
责编： 故渊
评论：
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，在今天（6 月 9 日）召开的 2026 全球开发者大会（WWDC）上，苹果公司面向开发者， 升级推出全新 Xcode，并同步推出 Core AI 框架。
苹果发布了新版 Foundation Models 框架，让开发者更灵活地调用 AI 能力。新框架已支持图像输入，开发者可把图片与文本一并传给设备端模型，让应用直接处理更丰富的多模态任务。
Xcode 方面，苹果把编码助手进一步推向智能体式编码工作流。新能力已覆盖应用本地化，帮助开发者处理多语言适配；编码助手也能与模拟设备交互，在编写代码，还能触达测试与调试相关环节。IT之家附上相关截图如下：
苹果还表示，开发者之后可更轻松地调整应用预览尺寸，并与预览界面交互。更多细节会在后续专题场次和 State of the Union 演讲中披露。
苹果软件工程高级副总裁 Craig Federighi 还称，Xcode 已成为构建智能体 AI 应用的“最佳场所”。
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

评论：
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，苹果今日向 Vision Pro 用户推送了 visionOS 27 开发者预览版 Beta 1 更新（内部版本号：24M5291p）。
苹果 visionOS 27 主要为海外机型引入了 Siri AI 功能，同步引入了全新视觉智能功能，可以看着场景询问 Siri。同时还支持将全景照片设置为个人环境。
需要注意的是，因苹果各区域节点服务器配置缓存问题，可能有些地方探测到升级更新的时间略有延迟，一般半小时内，不会太久。

title_original: 苹果 iOS / iPadOS 27 开发者预览版 Beta 1 发布：全新家长控制、Siri 集成相机等
body_original:
【 点此直达升级教程 】
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，苹果今日向 iPhone 和 iPad 用户推送了 iOS / iPadOS 27 开发者预览版 Beta 1 更新（内部版本号：24A5355q）。
如何升级 iOS / iPadOS / watchOS / macOS 开发版和公测版？
• 公开测试版 ：需注册 Apple Beta 版软件计划 ，之后通过【设置】【通用】【软件更新】【Beta 版更新】来升级；
• 开发预览版 ：需登录注册 苹果开发者计划 ，之后通过【设置】【通用】【软件更新】来升级。
附苹果 iOS 历史固件下载大全：
《 苹果 iOS / iPadOS / macOS 固件下载 / 更新日志大全 》
iOS 27 迎来多项性能优化，包括应用启动、显示照片、网络切换、AirDrop、硬盘读写等，以及 CPU 调度器升级。根据官方说法，升级 iOS 27 后，iPhone 和 iPad App 的打开速度最高提升 30%，新照片的显示速度最高提升 70%。
苹果还从底层重构聚焦、照片和邮件 App 中的搜索功能。在更新 iOS / iPad OS / macOS 27 后，系统会立刻开始为设备编制索引，改善搜索体验，而新增加的内容也会被立刻收入索引。
iCloud 功能也得到拓展，可以从 安卓 和 Windows 10 、 Windows 11 设备上添加照片到 iCloud 共享相册，并且支持全分辨率共享。
另外，iOS 27 引入了全新家长控制功能，新增儿童账号，家长可以便捷将新账号设置为“儿童账号”，家长可以在 App Store 自由查看每款应用是否适用于孩子，请求浏览和购买前询问对 13 周岁以下孩子默认开启。
IT之家注意到，在 iOS 27 中，Siri 已集成到 iPhone 的相机应用，可以识别物体并将其保存到 Siri 应用。在官方展示的案例中，用户可以在相机中启用 Siri，利用 AI 来记录摄入的饮食情况。在隐私保护方面，苹果表示会通过 Apple Intelligence 第二代设备端模型处理。
全新 Safari 浏览器能够检测打开的网页，自动整合标签页，便于用户查找，同时用户还可以用自然语言告诉 Safari 浏览器自己要关注的内容，内容更新时用户将会获得通知。苹果还基于 Siri AI 推出系统级别的 AI 自动校正（Automatic proofreading）功能。
iOS 27 系统还升级 Home 应用，通过 AI 技术来精简配件通知。对于部分 Home 应用来说，如果智能家居设备较多，就可能出现通知泛滥的情况，苹果公司希望通过 AI 来简化这些通知，减少 Home 生态对用户的干扰。此外，Home 应用可以识别已连接摄像头的视频片段并生成描述。
它还可以将来自不同摄像头的相关视频拼接在一起，用户还可以使用自然语言搜索视频片段。
iOS 27 照片 App 也升级了“空间构图”技术，苹果利用设备端空间模型和基于专用云计算的空间模型，让照片变身为“3D 空间场景”，用户可以后期自由放大、移动照片视角位置，支持所有照片，包括相机拍摄的照片。
除此之外，苹果还升级了扩图和背景杂物移除功能，进一步增强了相应功能效果。并改进图乐园功能，新增支持生成写实风格图片。
值得一提的是，苹果还官宣 iPhone 11 也能升级到 iOS 27 系统。
苹果 WWDC26 全球开发者大会专题

赛力斯“赛豆科技”全新品牌 AIVA 官宣：车标样式曝光，全系标配豆包大模型智能座舱
2026/6/9 7:13:44
来源： IT之家
作者： 汪淼
责编： 汪淼
评论：
感谢IT之家网友 热评钦定员 的线索投递！
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，赛豆科技品牌发布会定档 6 月 9 日 19:00-20:00 举行。根据官方直播页面，这一新汽车品牌定名 AIVA 。
根据此前爆料信息， 赛力斯正在推进一个全新汽车品牌的筹备工作 ，其法律主体正是刚刚完成工商变更的“赛豆科技”；新品牌定位年轻、运动， 首款车型预计今年内推出 。
爆料还称，赛力斯将与字节跳动旗下的云服务平台火山引擎展开深度合作，火山引擎在此次合作中的介入深度将超过此前与上汽荣威的“联合定义、联合开发”模式。
IT之家注意到， AIVA 品牌的车标已经曝光 ，出现在了微博广告页面。海报中可以看到 AIVA 首款车的尾部设计，采用红色车标样式，简洁字母设计。
博主 @新出行贺磊 表示，AIVA 首款车型全系标配豆包大模型智能座舱，智驾则和元戎启行联手。 首款车是纯电 + 增程双动力跨界车，介于轿车和 SUV 之间，定价 10-20 万 ，年内就会推出，还会自建渠道销往海内外。这次发布会，就是要见证 AI 和汽车融合的新玩法。
此外， 字节跳动官方 6 月 6 日发布声明称 ，赛豆不是字节跳动或豆包推出的汽车品牌，字节和赛豆没有股权合作。
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

苹果推出 Siri AI：独立 App，为灵动岛优化
2026/6/9 1:38:14
来源： IT之家
作者： 故渊
责编： 故渊
评论：
感谢IT之家网友 Wllen 、 长在红旗下 、 仿达 的线索投递！
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，在今天召开的 2026 年全球开发者大会（WWDC）上， 苹果克雷格 · 费德里吉（Craig Federighi）介绍推出了 Siri AI。
苹果称 Siri AI 是“全新版本的 Siri”，并表示它比之前的智能助手版本更具对话性，功能也更强大。在对话中，它的声音更加富有表现力，用户可以自定义语速、语气和口音。
Siri AI 支持在系统范围内使用，能够读取屏幕上的内容并与你的应用程序互动。但苹果公司软件工程高级副总裁克雷格 · 费德里吉表示，Siri 的设计“每一步都以隐私为重”。
并配有独立 App，屏幕顶部的灵动岛会冒出很大的气泡。
苹果迈克（Mike）正在向 Siri 询问一场演唱会以及如何获得门票，他要求 Siri 提醒他报名参加门票抽奖。
苹果迈克（Mike）要求它搜索他和孩子们一起旅行的照片：
IT之家援引博文介绍，苹果还大幅优化 Siri 的语音功能，用户可以通过语速和“表现力”来调整声音。
Siri 的这些更新也适用于 CarPlay 和 AirPods：
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

小米发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro UltraSpeed 模式，3 倍价格 10 倍输出体验
2026/6/9 0:26:37
来源： IT之家
作者： 沁沧（实习）
责编： 沁沧
评论：
感谢IT之家网友 软媒用户1392612 、 顺势而为 的线索投递！
IT之家 6 月 9 日消息，小米 MiMo 官方昨日宣布，与 TileRT 联合发布 Xiaomi MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 UltraSpeed 模式，通过模型与系统的极致 Codesign， 在通用 GPU 上将万亿参数模型的生成速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s 。
▲ 10 秒，做一个贪吃蛇小游戏 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed API 同步上线，采用限时体验价， 定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，同时提供输出速度约 10 倍的提升 （仅支持 API 体验，不支持 Token Plan）。官方称之为 3 倍价格提升，10 倍输出体验。
作为参考，小米 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 每百万 tokens 输入 0.025 元（缓存命中）/ 3 元（未命中缓存），每百万 tokens 输出 6 元。
▲ 1 分钟，复刻一个 macOS 系统 官方表示，由于高速推理资源供给有限，本次 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 采取申请制限时开放 ，申请通过的用户可限时接入 API 体验，时间仅限 2026 年 6 月 9 日至 6 月 23 日 23:59。
由于资源紧张，本次试用名额有限，提交申请后不承诺审核时效性和审核通过率；小米将优先审核具备真实业务需求的企业与专业开发者场景。
通过审核的用户可获得限时免费的 Chat 体验 （两周开放窗口内有效），为保障资源受限条件下的体验质量与使用公平性，试用服务规则如下：每个账号每日最多成功进入队列 10 次；单次会话时长上限 30 分钟；如会话空闲超过 5 分钟，系统将自动释放资源。
IT之家附相关链接如下：
申请入口： platform.xiaomimimo.com/ultraspeed
Chat 体验： ultraspeed.xiaomimimo.com
广告声明：文内含有的对外跳转链接（包括不限于超链接、二维码、口令等形式），用于传递更多信息，节省甄选时间，结果仅供参考，IT之家所有文章均包含本声明。

body_original:

🛒 YC's Shopping List: What the World's Top Accelerator Is Begging Founders to Build (Summer 2026)
A founder's field guide to Y Combinator's Request for Startups — decoded, ranked, mapped to real opportunities, and tracked across 17 years of evolution.
Primary Source: ycombinator.com/rfs — Summer 2026 Edition | Analysis compiled April 2026
TL;DR
Y Combinator's Request for Startups (RFS) is their public wishlist — the ideas they actively want founders to pitch. Think of it as a cheat code: you don't have to guess what YC wants to fund. They tell you.
The Summer 2026 edition marks a decisive shift. Gone are the broad sector labels ("Healthcare," "Defense," "Energy"). In their place: 16 hyper-specific bets — each written by a named YC partner, each reading like a mini-thesis on exactly why now is the moment for this company to exist.
The message: AI has stopped being a feature and started being the foundation. Now wire it into the physical world — silicon, agriculture, space, defense, and the operating system of every company on Earth.
📋 Table of Contents
The 16 Categories at a Glance
Deep Dive: Every Category Explained
How the RFS Has Evolved: 2009 → 2026
The Interdisciplinary Sweet Spots 🔀
Who's Already in the Arena: Customers & Competitors
Founder Backgrounds That Give You an Edge
The Build Difficulty Ranking: Weekend MVP to Moon Mission 🌙
Bonus: Ideas YC Didn't List But Should Have
Final Thoughts: How to Actually Use This List
The 16 Categories at a Glance

The count: 15 named categories + an overarching thesis statement. 7 are atoms-heavy, 7 are bits-heavy, 1 straddles both.
Deep Dive: Every Category Explained
1. 🌾 AI for Low-Pesticide Agriculture
Partner: Garry Tan (CEO) | Domain: AgTech × AI × Biotech × Robotics
The thesis: Modern agriculture is trapped in a doom loop — spray more chemicals → pests adapt → spray even more → margins collapse. AI vision, cheap sensors, precision robotics, and biological alternatives (microbes, peptides, RNA-based solutions) have converged to break this cycle. The company that cuts pesticide use by 90% and increases yields isn't just a good business — it's a generational company.
Key signals Garry highlights:
AI can now identify individual weeds and pests in real time
Cameras and sensors are cheap enough to blanket fields
Robots can treat one plant instead of spraying entire fields
Biology is catching up: engineered plants, microbial solutions, RNA interference
Agriculture is one of the biggest markets in the world
Why now: The convergence of computer vision, cheap hardware, and biological engineering makes precision agriculture viable at scale for the first time.
2. 💼 AI-Native Service Companies
Partner: Gustaf Alströmer | Domain: Enterprise Services × AI
The thesis: The evolution went: Services → SaaS → AI Copilots → AI does the work . Most 2023–2025 startups built copilots (tools that help people). YC now wants companies that skip the tool and just deliver the outcome . Total spend on services dwarfs software spend, and outsourced services are the easiest to replace.
Target verticals YC calls out:
Insurance brokerage
Accounting, tax, and audit
Compliance
Healthcare administration
Why now: AI capabilities crossed the threshold where "replace the service entirely" is more practical than "make the human 20% faster."
3. 🧬 AI Personalized Medicine
Partner: Ankit Gupta | Domain: Healthcare × Genomics × AI Agents
The thesis: Three revolutions are colliding: (1) AI agents can now analyze personalized health data with clinical-grade accuracy, (2) the cost of diagnostics (genome sequencing, biomarkers) is plummeting faster than Moore's Law, and (3) the cost of n-of-1 therapies (mRNA, gene therapies) is dropping while the FDA is becoming more open to letting patients try them.
The result: Truly personalized care delivery — not "here's what works on average" but "here's what works for you , based on your genome, your wearables data, and your medical history."
Why now: Agent harnesses like Claude Code can now analyze multi-modal health data. Genome sequencing costs have dropped below $200. FDA is signaling openness to personalized therapy pathways.
4. 🧠 Company Brain
Partner: Tom Blomfield | Domain: Enterprise Knowledge × AI Agents
The thesis: The biggest blocker to AI automation isn't the models — they're good enough. It's the domain knowledge . Every company's critical know-how is scattered across people's heads, Slack threads, old emails, support tickets, and databases. Humans vaguely remember where it is. AI agents can't operate like that.
What YC wants: A "company brain" — a living map of how a company works (how refunds are handled, how pricing exceptions are decided, how engineers respond to incidents) that AI agents can use as an "executable skills file."
This is NOT: A company-wide search tool or a chatbot over documents.
This IS: The missing layer between raw company data and reliable AI automation.
Why now: AI agents are ready to do real work, but they need structured knowledge to do it safely and consistently. Tom says every company in the world will need this.
5. 🛡️ Counter-Swarm Defense
Partner: Tyler Bosmeny | Domain: Defense × Hardware × AI × Real-time Systems
The thesis: "Last month, a swarm of cheap Iranian drones took out an AWS data center. Nobody stopped them." A Patriot missile costs $3M. An FPV drone costs $500. The cost advantage lies entirely with attackers, and current counter-drone defenses (radars, cameras, jammers, people with binoculars) won't work against coordinated swarms of hundreds.
What YC wants — the "counter-swarm stack":
High-capacity interceptors (one platform neutralizes 50 drones, not one)
Sensor fusion software (single real-time picture from all sensors)
Non-kinetic defenses (aerosols that foul rotors, streamers that entangle swarms)
Attacks on the autonomy stack itself (beyond radio jamming)
The killer quote: "Drone defense is looking less like operating a weapon and more like running a real-time distributed system. The winning companies will look more like Cloudflare than Raytheon."
Why now: Drone swarms have gone from theoretical to battlefield-proven. The Ukraine conflict, Houthi attacks, and the AWS incident have made this existential.
6. 💻 Dynamic Software Interfaces
Partner: Ankit Gupta | Domain: Developer Tools × AI × UX Design
The thesis: Before AI, all users got the same interface with minor customizations. But the way you use email is fundamentally different from how a college student uses email — yet every email client looks the same. Coding agents are now good enough that users can become their own "forward-deployed engineers," radically customizing the software they consume.
What YC envisions:
Software companies ship shared primitives, not finished UIs
Users' coding agents customize the final interface
Your email might look like a task list; a student's looks like an events calendar
The entire software delivery stack gets rethought: source code vs. binaries, frontend vs. middleware modification
Why now: AI coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, etc.) have made it possible for non-developers to meaningfully modify software interfaces.
7. 🚀 Electronics in Space
Partner: Philip Johnston | Domain: Aerospace × Semiconductor Design
The thesis: Reusable rockets (SpaceX, Stoke Space) are about to massively increase humanity's capacity to put things in space. That means enormous demand for space-grade compute — specifically inference chips optimized for mass, thermal management, and radiation hardness.
Target founder profile: "If you are working at SpaceX or NVIDIA and you've been spending time doing chip design, then Y Combinator would love to hear from you."
Why now: Launch costs are dropping exponentially. The bottleneck shifts from "getting to space" to "running workloads in space."
8. ⚙️ Hardware Supply Chain
Partner: Nicolas Dessaigne | Domain: Manufacturing × Logistics × Hardware
The thesis: Building hardware in the US is still far too slow compared to China. In Shenzhen, a team goes from design to new physical part in a day . In the US, that same loop takes weeks . That gap compounds with every iteration.
What YC wants:
Startups that produce parts dramatically faster
Tools enabling rapid hardware iteration
Tight integration of design, manufacturing, and logistics
YC companies already in this space: Hlabs (W26, building actuators for robots), Prototyping.io (P26, mechanical parts in days).
Why now: YC is funding more hardware companies than ever (robots, medical devices, space). Those companies all need a faster physical supply chain.
9. 🌙 Industrial Capabilities in Space
Partner: Adi Oltean | Domain: Aerospace × Manufacturing × Materials Science
The thesis: Develop industrial capabilities on the moon and in space — extracting raw materials (silicon, aluminum, iron, titanium) through electrolysis and 3D printing complex structures from molten regolith. Lunar manufacturing has structural advantages: no atmosphere means no support structures needed, lower gravity reduces energy costs.
Why now: Artemis program, commercial lunar landers (Intuitive Machines, Astrobotic), and reusable rockets are making lunar operations increasingly feasible.
10. 🔧 Inference Chips for Agent Workflows
Partner: Diana Hu | Domain: Semiconductor Design × AI Infrastructure
The thesis: Current AI chips are designed for "prompt in, response out." But agents don't work that way — they loop, call tools, branch, backtrack, and hold context across dozens of steps. Current GPUs hit only 30–40% utilization on agentic workloads because the work is bursty.
What's needed:
Fast context switching between models
Native speculative decoding
Memory built for KV caches that persist across entire execution graphs
Compilers that understand the agent loop (Groq's real insight was the compiler, not the chip)
Context: NVIDIA acquired Groq for $20B. Google built TPU v7 for inference. But nobody's designing for the agent loop itself .
Why now: Agentic AI has moved from research demo to production workload. The hardware isn't keeping up.
11. ⚔️ SaaS Challengers
Partner: Jared Friedman | Domain: Enterprise Software × AI
The thesis: "Everyone's talking about how AI coding means the end of SaaS. Investors have wiped trillions off software market caps. Well, that might be bad news for incumbents, but it's good news for startups ."
AI has collapsed the cost of producing software by 10–100×. The moat that protected legacy SaaS (millions of lines of code built over decades) is gone.
Attack vectors Jared suggests:
Clone an existing product, sell it for 1/10th the price
Build AI-native from the ground up (not a chatbot bolted onto a 2010 UI)
Bundle 10 point solutions into one suite
Open-source a $50K/seat product, monetize through services
His challenge: "Don't go after project management tools. Go after the products that seem invulnerable: chip design software, ERPs, industrial control systems, supply chain management. The giant, 10-million-line codebases that have been untouchable for decades."
Why now: AI coding has made the "million lines of code" moat obsolete. The incumbents are vulnerable for the first time in decades.
12. 🤖 Software for Agents
Partner: Aaron Epstein | Domain: Developer Tools × AI Infrastructure
The thesis: "The next trillion users on the internet won't be people — they'll be AI agents." Right now, agents interact with software designed for humans (clicking buttons in browsers), which is slow, inconsistent, and brittle.
What agents actually need:
Machine-readable interfaces (APIs, MCPs, CLIs) instead of visual ones (forms, buttons)
Thorough documentation for programmatic discovery
The ability to sign up for and use tools without human-in-the-loop
The framing: "While everyone else is building agents, the biggest opportunity might be building the software those agents depend on."
Why now: Agent usage has crossed the threshold where human-designed UIs are the bottleneck, not the models.
13. 🏢 Startups Selling to Huge Companies
Partners: Harshita Arora & Brad Flora | Domain: Enterprise Sales × AI
The thesis: PG's classic advice was "sell to other startups." But AI has unlocked a new playbook: sell directly to Fortune 100 companies from Day 1.
Three things changed:
Buyers are awake. F100 leaders are actively looking for AI teams. YC companies are landing multi-million dollar deals during the batch .
Small teams ship fast. A 2–3 person team can now ship something a Fortune 10 finds useful before the ink is dry on incorporation docs.
Leaders understand urgency. They know what to build internally, what to outsource, and what happens if they don't adapt.
Why now: Enterprise buyers are proactively seeking startups for the first time. The "stealth for 3 years until feature parity" meme is dead.
14. 🔗 Supply Chain 2.0 for Semiconductors
Partner: Diana Hu | Domain: Semiconductors × Supply Chain × Software
The thesis: A single advanced AI chip crosses ~1,400 process steps, a dozen countries, and takes five months to build. This supply chain is managed with spreadsheets, SAP, and phone calls.
The pain:
In 2021, a $300 chip held up $50,000 cars; $210B in vehicles didn't get built
TSMC's advanced packaging is the single biggest bottleneck in AI compute
NVIDIA has locked up 60%+ of it
HBM memory is booked through 2026
Export controls change quarterly
CHIPS Act fabs in Arizona, Texas, Ohio, New York each need supply chains built from scratch
Why now: "You need to understand wafer allocation and packaging constraints at a deep level to build this, which is exactly why it's a startup opportunity and not a feature inside SAP."
15. 🏭 The AI Operating System for Companies
Partner: Diana Hu | Domain: Enterprise AI × Operations
The thesis: The best AI-native companies have made their entire company queryable — every meeting recorded, every ticket tracked, every customer interaction captured, all legible to an intelligence layer that learns from it. This turns a company from an open loop (make a decision, check results weeks later) into a closed loop (system monitors, compares to targets, adjusts automatically).
The gap: Building this today requires brutal integration work — stitching together Slack, Linear, GitHub, Notion, call recordings, and a dozen other tools with custom glue code. There's no product that connects all this context into a single intelligence layer.
Why now: The tools exist (transcription, LLMs, embeddings), but nobody has built the connective layer that makes a company legible to AI by default.
How the RFS Has Evolved: 2009 → 2026
The RFS isn't new — it dates back to Paul Graham in 2009 , not Sam Altman in 2014 as commonly believed. Understanding how it evolved reveals what YC really cares about vs. what's trend-chasing.
The Three Eras

🛒 YC's Shopping List: What the World's Top Accelerator Is Begging Founders to Build (Summer 2026)
A founder's field guide to Y Combinator's Request for Startups — decoded, ranked, mapped to real opportunities, and tracked across 17 years of evolution.
Primary Source: ycombinator.com/rfs — Summer 2026 Edition | Analysis compiled April 2026
TL;DR
Y Combinator's Request for Startups (RFS) is their public wishlist — the ideas they actively want founders to pitch. Think of it as a cheat code: you don't have to guess what YC wants to fund. They tell you.
The Summer 2026 edition marks a decisive shift. Gone are the broad sector labels ("Healthcare," "Defense," "Energy"). In their place: 16 hyper-specific bets — each written by a named YC partner, each reading like a mini-thesis on exactly why now is the moment for this company to exist.
The message: AI has stopped being a feature and started being the foundation. Now wire it into the physical world — silicon, agriculture, space, defense, and the operating system of every company on Earth.
📋 Table of Contents

Deep Dive: Every Category Explained
How the RFS Has Evolved: 2009 → 2026

Who's Already in the Arena: Customers & Competitors

The Build Difficulty Ranking: Weekend MVP to Moon Mission 🌙
Bonus: Ideas YC Didn't List But Should Have

The thesis: The best AI-native companies have made their entire company queryable — every meeting recorded, every ticket tracked, every customer interaction captured, all legible to an intelligence layer that learns from it. This turns a company from an open loop (make a decision, check results weeks later) into a closed loop (system monitors, compares to targets, adjusts automatically).
The gap: Building this today requires brutal integration work — stitching together Slack, Linear, GitHub, Notion, call recordings, and a dozen other tools with custom glue code. There's no product that connects all this context into a single intelligence layer.
Why now: The tools exist (transcription, LLMs, embeddings), but nobody has built the connective layer that makes a company legible to AI by default.
How the RFS Has Evolved: 2009 → 2026
The RFS isn't new — it dates back to Paul Graham in 2009 , not Sam Altman in 2014 as commonly believed. Understanding how it evolved reveals what YC really cares about vs. what's trend-chasing.
The Three Eras
Era
Period
Format
Architect
Philosophy
1. The Numbered Essays
2009–2012
Individual pages (rfs1.html, rfs2.html...)
Paul Graham
Specific product niches ("Kill Hollywood," "Future of Journalism")
2. The Broad Sectors
2014–2023
Single consolidated page, ~12-27 broad categories
Sam Altman → Kat Mañalac
"Breakthrough tech" sectors (Energy, AI, Robotics, Healthcare)
3. The Rolling Specifics
2024–Present
Seasonal editions, ~6-20 specific bets, named partners
Dalton Caldwell
Hyper-specific opportunities ("Counter-Swarm Defense," "Inference Chips for Agent Workflows")
The Full Timeline: Every Major RFS Edition
2009 ──── PG publishes YCRFS 1-5 (Journalism, Mobile, Twitter, Live Video)
2010 ──── YCRFS 9: "Kill Hollywood" (2,060 HN points — most famous RFS ever)
2014 ──── Sam Altman consolidates into single page: 12 categories
│ Energy | AI | Robotics | Biotech | Healthcare | Food & Water
│ Education | Internet Infrastructure | Transportation & Housing
│ Levers | Science | Elsewhere
│ ➕ "One Million Jobs" added mid-cycle
2016 ──── Major refresh: 22 categories (+Enterprise SW, Financial Services,
│ VR/AR, Programming Tools, Computer Security, Diversity, and more)
2018 ──── Updated: 25 categories (+Carbon Removal, Longevity, Clean Meat,
│ Future of Work, Cellular Agriculture; −Hollywood 2.0, Mass Media)
2020 ──── +COVID-19 Response, +Government 2.0 (27 categories)
2022 ──── Trimmed to 19 (dropped Computer Security, Voice Apps, others)
│ ═══════ 6-year static period ends ═══════
2024 S ─── BREAKING CHANGE: Dalton Caldwell throws out everything.
│ 20 hyper-specific categories. Named partners.
│ (ML for Robotics, New Defense Tech, Stablecoin Finance,
│ End Cancer, Small Fine-tuned Models, and more)
2025 W ─── 9 categories. Post-election: GovTech, Public Safety,
│ Manufacture in USA, Stablecoins 2.0, LLMs for Chip Design
│ REVIVAL: "One Million Jobs 2.0" (back from 2014!)
2025 Sp ──

Meta
Growth Insight
Meta Platforms reported a first-quarter revenue of $56.31 billion for 2026, reflecting a 33% increase year over year from approximately $42.34 billion. This growth was driven by a 19% rise in ad impressions and a 12% increase in average ad prices. The company also generated a net income of $26.8 billion, marking a 61% increase compared to the previous year. Despite these positive financial results, Meta faced challenges, including a decline in daily active users across its family of apps, which fell to 3.56 billion from 3.58 billion in the prior quarter, marking the first decline since 2019. Additionally, Meta announced plans to lay off about 8,000 employees, roughly 10% of its workforce, as part of a restructuring to focus on AI investments and efficiency improvements. The company also completed a $2 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus, although this deal was later ordered to be unwound by Chinese regulators due to national security concerns.
